title: OpenAI Replaces ChatGPT's Default Model with GPT-5.5 Instant
summary: OpenAI launched GPT-5.5 Instant as ChatGPT's new default model, focusing on fewer hallucinations, tighter responses, and better personalization for Plus and Pro subscribers.
content: OpenAI has replaced the default ChatGPT model with GPT-5.5 Instant, an incremental upgrade built for factual accuracy and conciseness over its predecessor GPT-5.3 Instant. The model scored 81.2 on the AIME 2025 math benchmark — up from 65.4 for GPT-5.3 — and shows measurable improvements across multimodal reasoning tasks. GPT-5.5 Instant can draw on past conversations, uploaded files, and Gmail data to generate more tailored responses, a feature rolling out first to Plus and Pro users on the web. OpenAI is also introducing 'memory sources' across all ChatGPT users, providing transparency into what information influenced a response, with options to correct or delete saved memories. The previous GPT-5.3 model will remain available as a paid option for three months before retirement.

title: EU Reaches Deal to Simplify AI Act and Ban Nudifier Apps
summary: European Parliament and Council negotiators struck a provisional deal to streamline AI Act compliance timelines and ban AI systems used to generate non-consensual intimate imagery.
content: EU co-legislators reached a provisional agreement early Thursday to amend the AI Act as part of the Digital Omnibus package, delaying high-risk AI obligations and simplifying compliance for businesses. High-risk AI rules — covering biometrics, critical infrastructure, education, employment, and law enforcement — will now apply from December 2027 instead of August 2026, giving companies time to prepare as technical standards are finalized. The deal also bans AI systems that generate non-consensual intimate imagery or child sexual abuse material, with companies given until December 2026 to comply. Machinery product AI will only need to meet sectoral safety rules rather than both the AI Act and sectoral rules, addressing longstanding concerns from German industry heavyweights including Siemens and Bosch. The provisional agreement must be formally adopted by both Parliament and Council before August 2026 to prevent legal uncertainty for companies currently subject to the existing high-risk rules.

title: All Five Major AI Labs Now Submit Models for US Government Pre-Release Review
summary: Google, Microsoft, and xAI joined OpenAI and Anthropic in giving the US Commerce Department voluntary pre-release access to evaluate frontier AI models, creating the closest thing America has to an AI oversight system.
content: The Commerce Department's Center for AI Standards and Innovation announced that Google, Microsoft, and xAI will now submit their AI models for government evaluation before public release, joining OpenAI and Anthropic in a voluntary arrangement covering all five major frontier AI labs. The expansion was catalyzed by Anthropic's Mythos model — which can autonomously discover and exploit zero-day vulnerabilities across operating systems — alarming national security officials and prompting senior White House involvement. CAISI has completed more than 40 evaluations of AI models since 2024, including unreleased state-of-the-art systems, though the office has fewer than 200 staff and no statutory authority to delay or block a deployment. The agreements are voluntary commitments that companies can withdraw at any time, widely seen as a hedge against legislation that would give the center mandatory evaluation powers. The Trump administration is reportedly considering an executive order that could formalize the review process with regulatory teeth, transforming the current voluntary system into something binding.

title: AWS Launches Agent Toolkit to Help AI Coding Agents Build on Cloud Services
summary: Amazon Web Services released the Agent Toolkit for AWS, a production-ready suite of over 40 skills, a managed MCP server, and installable plugins designed to make AI coding agents more accurate and governable on AWS.
content: AWS launched the Agent Toolkit for AWS on May 6, addressing a persistent problem where AI coding agents struggle with complex multi-service workflows and rely on outdated knowledge of cloud services. The toolkit includes over 40 'agent skills' — validated, up-to-date procedures for tasks like authoring CloudFormation templates, configuring data pipelines, and building serverless applications — so agents follow proven best practices rather than improvising. The AWS MCP Server, now generally available, allows coding agents to interact with any AWS service with IAM-based guardrails, CloudWatch and CloudTrail observability, and sandboxed code execution for multi-step operations. Three pre-built agent plugins bundle the MCP server with curated skills: AWS Core for full-stack apps, AWS Data Analytics for data pipelines, and AWS Agents for building production-ready agents on Amazon Bedrock AgentCore. The toolkit carries no additional charge — customers pay only for the underlying AWS resources their agents consume.

title: Tableau Transforms into an Agentic Analytics Platform for the Fortune 100
summary: Salesforce's Tableau unveiled a major platform evolution that turns its analytics tool into a knowledge and decision engine capable of powering autonomous AI agents across the enterprise.
content: Tableau unveiled its Agentic Analytics Platform on May 5, repositioning the tool trusted by 97% of the Fortune 100 from a visualization product into what the company calls a 'high-scale knowledge and decision engine' for the agentic enterprise. At the core is a Knowledge Engine built on 33 million semantic models contributed by the Tableau community over a decade, giving AI agents verified business context — metrics, relationships, definitions — rather than raw data guesses. New headless analytics capabilities deliver insights directly into Slack, Microsoft Teams, Google Workspace, and other tools via open MCP servers, so agents and employees get trusted data wherever work happens without opening a dashboard. A Decision Engine closes the loop between insight and action, automatically triggering workflows such as creating Salesforce cases or alerting team leads when performance metrics breach thresholds. Tableau Agent conversational analytics are generally available now, while the Agentic Analytics Command Center for enterprise-wide governance is expected in the fall.

title: ServiceNow Launches Otto, a Cross-Enterprise AI Agent That Completes Work End-to-End
summary: ServiceNow introduced Otto, a unified AI experience combining its Now Assist and Moveworks technology to autonomously handle employee and customer requests across every department and enterprise system.
content: ServiceNow announced Otto at its Knowledge 2026 conference, positioning it as a solution to enterprise AI's 'completion problem' — where chatbots answer questions but fail to finish multi-step work spanning departments. Otto combines Moveworks conversational AI with ServiceNow's orchestration platform, understanding employee intent and routing requests to completion without requiring users to know which system handles each task. The platform supports natural language requests, enterprise search across SharePoint and wikis, AI voice agents with no hold queues or menu trees, and a plain-language data explorer for business analysis. Every action is governed by ServiceNow's AI Control Tower, which logs each interaction, enforces enterprise policies, and provides explainability for decisions — giving IT teams oversight as agents scale. Just one month after launching EmployeeWorks, the first product powered by Otto, ServiceNow closed six deals exceeding $1 million each in net new annual contract value.
